content.content:
1	/*
2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	import "time"
20	
21	// APIVersionV1Alpha1 is the envelope contract version shipped in TIDE v1alpha1.
22	// Consumers MUST reject envelopes whose apiVersion field does not match this
23	// constant via [ValidateAPIVersionKind].
24	const APIVersionV1Alpha1 = "tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1"
25	
26	// KindTaskEnvelopeIn is the "kind" discriminator for input envelopes written
27	// by the orchestrator and consumed by subagent images.
28	const KindTaskEnvelopeIn = "TaskEnvelopeIn"
29	
30	// KindTaskEnvelopeOut is the "kind" discriminator for output envelopes written
31	// by the in-pod harness and consumed by the controller on Job completion.
32	const KindTaskEnvelopeOut = "TaskEnvelopeOut"
33	
34	// EnvelopeIn is the self-contained input document written by the orchestrator
35	// to /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/in.json on the per-Project PVC. The
36	// subagent pod mounts the PVC read-only; everything the agent needs to run is
37	// in this document (D-A4). Out-of-tree subagent image authors decode this type
38	// via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] + json.Unmarshal.
39	type EnvelopeIn struct {
40		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1]. Harness MUST reject any
41		// other value via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] before consuming any field.
42		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
43	
44		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeIn]. Harness MUST reject any other value.
45		Kind string `json:"kind"`
46	
47		// TaskUID is the Kubernetes UID of the owning Task CRD object. Carried
48		// through to EnvelopeOut so the controller correlates result envelopes
49		// without scanning the PVC directory tree.
50		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
51	
52		// Role is the planner/executor selector: "planner" or "executor".
53		Role string `json:"role"`
54	
55		// Level is the hierarchy level this task operates at:
56		// "milestone" | "phase" | "plan" | "task".
57		Level string `json:"level"`
58	
59		// Prompt is the full prompt body the subagent should act on.
60		// For executor dispatches this field is empty — the executor reads the
61		// prompt in-pod from PromptPath off its own namespace PVC (defect #10b).
62		// For planner dispatches (role="planner") this field carries the resolved
63		// outcome prompt directly (the outcome prompt is not a PVC artifact).
64		Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
65	
66		// PromptPath is the workspace-relative path of the executor prompt artifact
67		// on the per-Project namespace PVC (e.g.
68		// "envelopes/<plannerUID>/children/task-01.json"). Non-empty only for
69		// executor dispatches (role="executor"). The in-pod anthropic runner reads
70		// the .spec.prompt field from this artifact and renders it as {{.Prompt}}
71		// in the compiled-in prompt template. The Manager MUST NOT read this path
72		// cross-namespace; the read happens in-pod so it uses the executor's own
73		// namespace PVC (defect #10b). Traversal-defended in-pod: absolute paths
74		// and "../" escapes are rejected before the read.
75		PromptPath string `json:"promptPath,omitempty"`
76	
77		// Branch is the per-run git branch (project.Status.Git.BranchName, format
78		// "tide/run-<project>-<unix>") the executor checks out its isolated worktree
79		// onto. Non-empty only for executor dispatches (role="executor"); planner
80		// dispatches short-circuit worktree creation (harness.EnsureWorktree). The
81		// in-pod executor reads this from in.json — the single source of truth for
82		// the worktree branch (09-09: replaced the never-written branch.txt).
83		Branch string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
84	
85		// FilesTouched is the set of repo-relative paths this task is expected to
86		// read or write. Used by the harness for output-path validation (HARN-05)
87		// and by the Plan admission webhook for file-touch consistency checks
88		// (PLAN-02).
89		FilesTouched []string `json:"filesTouched"`
90	
91		// DependsOn is the list of sibling Task names this task declares as
92		// predecessors in the execution DAG. Omitted when empty (D-A4).
93		DependsOn []string `json:"dependsOn,omitempty"`
94	
95		// DeclaredOutputPaths is the exhaustive list of PVC-relative paths the
96		// harness is permitted to write. Any write outside this set is rejected by
97		// the harness (HARN-05).
98		DeclaredOutputPaths []string `json:"declaredOutputPaths"`
99	
100		// Caps carries per-task resource limits enforced by the in-pod harness
101		// (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives these from Project.Spec.budget.
102		Caps Caps `json:"caps"`
103	
104		// ProxyEndpoint is the localhost HTTPS endpoint for the signed-token
105		// credential proxy sidecar (D-C1). Subagent sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to
106		// this value; the sidecar holds the real API key.
107		ProxyEndpoint string `json:"proxyEndpoint"`
108	
109		// SignedToken is the HMAC-SHA256 token minted by the controller at
110		// Job-create time. The credential proxy validates this token before
111		// forwarding API requests (D-C3).
112		SignedToken string `json:"signedToken"`
113	
114		// Provider selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
115		// subagent image should use to satisfy this dispatch (D-C3). Value type
116		// (not pointer): every dispatch declares a provider. The dispatching
117		// reconciler resolves Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,
118		// params} → Project-level defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the
119		// resolved triple here. The provider implementation in
120		// internal/subagent/{vendor}/ rejects an envelope whose Provider.Vendor
121		// does not match its compiled-in sentinel — fail-fast defense against
122		// image-tag-vs-envelope drift.
123		//
124		// Provider.Params carries MODEL parameters only (temperature, thinking_budget,
125		// …). It is NOT a general metadata bag — the anthropic runner hard-rejects
126		// any key outside its allow-list. Dispatch metadata the planner needs to
127		// build child-CRD refs lives in [EnvelopeIn.Dispatch], not here.
128		Provider ProviderSpec `json:"provider"`
129	
130		// Dispatch carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
131		// reads to build child-CRD references (e.g. parentName → projectRef /
132		// milestoneRef / phaseRef / planRef) without querying the K8s API. It is
133		// distinct from Provider.Params, which is model-parameters-only: overloading
134		// Provider.Params with dispatch metadata tripped the anthropic runner's
135		// strict param allow-list. Pointer + omitempty so executor-level and
136		// real-Claude dispatches that don't consume it don't serialize a
137		// "dispatch: null" placeholder. Real Claude-backed subagent images may
138		// ignore this struct entirely — the real planner authors from the outcome
139		// prompt, not canned parent refs.
140		Dispatch *DispatchMeta `json:"dispatch,omitempty"`
141	
142		// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata injected by integration tests.
143		// Real Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely
144		// (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9 / D-F1). The field is omitted from JSON when nil
145		// so production envelopes are not polluted with "dev: null".
146		Dev *Dev `json:"dev,omitempty"`
147	}
148	
149	// EnvelopeOut is the result document written by the in-pod harness to
150	// /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/out.json. The controller reads this file
151	// after the K8s Job terminates to record Task completion status and budget
152	// tally (D-A2).
153	type EnvelopeOut struct {
154		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1].
155		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
156	
157		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeOut].
158		Kind string `json:"kind"`
159	
160		// TaskUID mirrors EnvelopeIn.TaskUID so the controller can correlate
161		// result envelopes without scanning directory names.
162		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
163	
164		// ExitCode is the subagent process exit code: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
165		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
166	
167		// Result is a human-readable one-line summary of the task outcome. Written
168		// by the harness on success or by the stub's canned response on testMode.
169		Result string `json:"result"`
170	
171		// Reason carries a structured failure code when ExitCode != 0, e.g.
172		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit", "output-path-violation", "token-expired".
173		Reason string `json:"reason"`
174	
175		// Usage is the token and cost tally for this task, rolled up by the
176		// controller into Project.Status.budget at Task completion (D-D2).
177		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
178	
179		// Artifacts is the list of PVC-relative paths the harness confirmed were
180		// written during this task's execution. Used for audit and for the
181		// declared-vs-actual output-path comparison (HARN-05).
182		Artifacts []string `json:"artifacts"`
183	
184		// CompletedAt is the wall-clock time the harness wrote this envelope.
185		CompletedAt time.Time `json:"completedAt"`
186	
187		// ChildCRDs is the slice of typed child-CRD declarations a planner
188		// subagent emits for the orchestrator to materialize server-side (D-A1).
189		// MilestoneReconciler reads Phase entries here; PhaseReconciler reads
190		// Plan entries; PlanReconciler reads Task + Wave entries; TaskReconciler
191		// (executor level) emits no ChildCRDs. Omitted from JSON when empty so
192		// executor-level EnvelopeOut documents stay small. Consumers MUST
193		// validate ChildCRDSpec.Kind against an allowlist before server-side
194		// create — threat T-308 mitigation lives at the consumer site (plan
195		// 03-08), not in this type.
196		ChildCRDs []ChildCRDSpec `json:"childCRDs,omitempty"`
197	
198		// Git carries git-side output a push Job or a harness produces, in
199		// particular the HeadSHA the per-Project run branch was advanced to at
200		// this dispatch's level boundary. Pointer + omitempty so dispatches that
201		// don't touch git (e.g. planner Jobs whose output is consumed only via
202		// child-CRD materialization) don't serialize a "git: null" placeholder.
203		Git *GitOutput `json:"git,omitempty"`
204	
205		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored. When
206		// reading the tiny termination-message status (PodStatusEnvelopeReader),
207		// this field is populated from the TerminationStub.ChildCount JSON key so
208		// planner controllers can perform race-free succession gating without also
209		// carrying the full ChildCRDs payloads (plan 09-08 Defect B). Zero for
210		// executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners. When reading a full
211		// out.json via FilesystemEnvelopeReader the field falls back to
212		// len(ChildCRDs) in the planner controllers (non-Option-C path).
213		ChildCount int `json:"childCount,omitempty"`
214	}
215	
216	// GitOutput carries git-side output fields a dispatch produces, populated by
217	// the push Job at level-boundary push success and by executor harnesses
218	// committing per-Task worktree changes (D-B4 / D-B6). HeadSHA is the SHA the
219	// per-run branch was advanced to; ProjectReconciler patches
220	// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA from this value to enable the next push's
221	// --force-with-lease check (Pitfall 13 mitigation).
222	type GitOutput struct {
223		// HeadSHA is the 40-character hex SHA the per-run branch points to after
224		// this dispatch's level-boundary push completed. Required when Git is
225		// non-nil.
226		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA"`
227	}
228	
229	// Caps carries per-task resource limits (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives
230	// these from Project.Spec.budget and injects them into every EnvelopeIn so
231	// the in-pod harness can enforce them without querying the K8s API.
232	type Caps struct {
233		// WallClockSeconds is the maximum wall-clock seconds the subagent is
234		// permitted to run before the harness sends SIGTERM.
235		WallClockSeconds int `json:"wallClockSeconds"`
236	
237		// Iterations is the maximum number of agent loop iterations before the
238		// harness sends SIGTERM.
239		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
240	
241		// InputTokens is the maximum cumulative input tokens across all LLM calls.
242		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
243	
244		// OutputTokens is the maximum cumulative output tokens across all LLM
245		// calls in this task.
246		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
247	}
248	
249	// Usage carries the token and cost tally reported by the harness in
250	// EnvelopeOut. Rolled up by the controller into Project.Status.budget at Task
251	// completion (D-D2).
252	type Usage struct {
253		// InputTokens is the total input tokens consumed across all LLM calls
254		// during this task's execution.
255		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
256	
257		// OutputTokens is the total output tokens produced across all LLM calls
258		// during this task's execution.
259		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
260	
261		// EstimatedCostCents is the estimated US-cent cost for this task, rounded
262		// up to the nearest cent. Used for Project.Status.budget.costSpentCents
263		// rollup.
264		EstimatedCostCents int64 `json:"estimatedCostCents"`
265	
266		// Iterations is the actual number of agent loop iterations completed.
267		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
268	
269		// CacheReadTokens is the count of input tokens served from the provider's
270		// prompt cache (D-C5). The Anthropic stream-json
271		// usage.cache_read_input_tokens field maps directly here. Surfacing this
272		// separately lets Project.Status.budget (Phase 2 D-D2) show the cached
273		// vs uncached input mix instead of hiding cache hits inside InputTokens.
274		CacheReadTokens int64 `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
275	
276		// CacheCreationTokens is the count of input tokens spent populating the
277		// provider's prompt cache during this dispatch (D-C5). The Anthropic
278		// stream-json usage.cache_creation_input_tokens field maps directly here.
279		// Cache creation is billed at a higher rate than cache reads; separating
280		// the two prevents budget rollups from understating real spend on
281		// cache-warmup turns.
282		CacheCreationTokens int64 `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
283	}
284	
285	// DispatchMeta carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
286	// reads to build child-CRD references without querying the K8s API. It is kept
287	// separate from [ProviderSpec.Params] (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic
288	// runner's strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata. Real
289	// Claude-backed subagent images may ignore this struct — the real planner
290	// authors child CRDs from the outcome prompt, not canned parent refs.
291	type DispatchMeta struct {
292		// ParentName is the up-stack CRD's metadata.name. The stub planner stamps
293		// it into the child CRD's parent-spec ref (projectRef / milestoneRef /
294		// phaseRef / planRef) so the controller can wire ownership without the
295		// subagent holding a K8s client (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a
296		// secret).
297		ParentName string `json:"parentName,omitempty"`
298	}
299	
300	// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata for the stub-subagent image. Real
301	// Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely (D-F1 /
302	// RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9). It is intentionally kept in a sub-struct so the
303	// compiler makes omission explicit (pointer + omitempty on EnvelopeIn.Dev) and
304	// grep for "Dev" in production code is unambiguous.
305	type Dev struct {
306		// TestMode is the stub behavior selector. Valid values:
307		//   "success"              — writes canned result.json + declared artifacts; exit 0.
308		//   "fail-exit-1"          — writes structured failure result; exit 1.
309		//   "hang"                 — sleeps past caps.wallClockSeconds (HARN-02 test).
310		//   "exceed-output-paths"  — writes outside declaredOutputPaths (HARN-05 test).
311		// An empty string is treated as "success" by the stub.
312		TestMode string `json:"testMode,omitempty"`
313	}
314	
315	// TerminationStub is the tiny cross-namespace status carrier written to the
316	// dispatch Job's 4 KB termination message (Pod.status.containerStatuses[].
317	// state.terminated.message). It carries ONLY the fields the Manager needs to
318	// record Task completion without reading the PVC: ExitCode, Reason, Usage,
319	// the HeadSHA the run-branch was advanced to (if any), and ChildCount — the
320	// count of child CRDs the planner authored. ChildCRDs payloads and the verbose
321	// Result are intentionally excluded — they stay on the namespace-local PVC in
322	// out.json, which the in-namespace reporter Job reads (plan 09-05).
323	//
324	// ChildCount enables race-free succession gating (plan 09-08 Defect B): the
325	// controller compares observed owned children against this expected count and
326	// requeues until observed >= expected before declaring the level Succeeded.
327	//
328	// By construction this type MUST stay < 4 KB when marshalled; the test
329	// TestNewTerminationStub_StaysSmall enforces this invariant.
330	type TerminationStub struct {
331		// ExitCode mirrors EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
332		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
333	
334		// Reason mirrors EnvelopeOut.Reason: structured failure code (e.g.
335		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit") when ExitCode != 0.
336		Reason string `json:"reason"`
337	
338		// Usage mirrors EnvelopeOut.Usage: the token and cost tally for this
339		// dispatch, rolled up by the Manager into Project.Status.budget.
340		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
341	
342		// HeadSHA is the git HeadSHA from EnvelopeOut.Git.HeadSHA, flattened here
343		// to avoid a nested pointer. Empty when the dispatch did not produce a git
344		// push (executor-level, non-push Jobs). The Manager copies this into
345		// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA for the --force-with-lease fence.
346		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA,omitempty"`
347	
348		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored
349		// (len(EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs)). Used by the four planner-level controllers
350		// as the "expected" child count for race-free succession gating: a level
351		// cannot Succeed until observed owned children >= ChildCount (plan 09-08).
352		// Zero for executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners.
353		ChildCount int `json:"childCount"`
354	}
355	
356	// NewTerminationStub builds a TerminationStub from a full EnvelopeOut by
357	// copying the tiny-status subset: ExitCode, Reason, Usage, Git.HeadSHA, and
358	// ChildCount (= len(out.ChildCRDs)). ChildCRD payloads, Result, and Artifacts
359	// are deliberately excluded. A nil out.Git is safe and produces an empty
360	// HeadSHA. A nil or empty ChildCRDs slice produces ChildCount == 0.
361	func NewTerminationStub(out EnvelopeOut) TerminationStub {
362		headSHA := ""
363		if out.Git != nil {
364			headSHA = out.Git.HeadSHA
365		}
366		return TerminationStub{
367			ExitCode:   out.ExitCode,
368			Reason:     out.Reason,
369			Usage:      out.Usage,
370			HeadSHA:    headSHA,
371			ChildCount: len(out.ChildCRDs),
372		}
373	}
374	
375	// ValidateAPIVersionKind checks that apiVersion equals [APIVersionV1Alpha1]
376	// and that kind equals expectedKind. It is the first call an envelope consumer
377	// must make before accessing any other field (D-A3).
378	//
379	// Returns [*UnknownAPIVersionError] if apiVersion is unrecognized.
380	// Returns [*UnknownKindError] if kind is unrecognized.
381	// Returns nil on success.
382	func ValidateAPIVersionKind(apiVersion, kind, expectedKind string) error {
383		if apiVersion != APIVersionV1Alpha1 {
384			return &UnknownAPIVersionError{APIVersion: apiVersion}
385		}
386		if kind != expectedKind {
387			return &UnknownKindError{Kind: kind}
388		}
389		return nil
390	}
391
v1alpha1

tool_use_result.stdout: v1alpha1

1	/*

3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	// ProviderSpec selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
20	// subagent image should use to satisfy a single dispatch (D-C3). It is carried
21	// on every EnvelopeIn so vendor + model are orthogonal axes from the
22	// orchestrator's point of view: the dispatching reconciler resolves
23	// Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,params} → Project-level
24	// defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the resolved triple here. The
25	// provider-side Subagent implementation (e.g. internal/subagent/anthropic)
26	// reads Provider.Model to drive its CLI invocation and rejects an envelope
27	// whose Provider.Vendor does not match the image's compiled-in sentinel —
28	// fail-fast defense against config drift between image tag and envelope
29	// content.
30	//
31	// Params is intentionally a string→string map (not a typed struct) so future
32	// per-vendor knobs (Anthropic thinking-budget, OpenAI temperature, Google
33	// safetySettings, etc.) can land without a pkg/dispatch schema bump every
34	// time. Validation of Params keys/values is the provider implementation's
35	// responsibility — pkg/dispatch only guarantees the bytes round-trip.
36	type ProviderSpec struct {
37		// Vendor is the provider sentinel string the subagent image checks at
38		// startup. Canonical values: "anthropic", "openai", "google", "xai",
39		// "opencode". Required (no omitempty) — every dispatch declares a vendor.
40		Vendor string `json:"vendor"`
41	
42		// Model is the per-vendor model identifier passed to the provider CLI or
43		// SDK (e.g. "claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5",
44		// "o1-mini"). Required (no omitempty).
45		Model string `json:"model"`
46	
47		// Params is the per-vendor tuning passthrough. Keys and values are
48		// vendor-specific; pkg/dispatch performs no semantic validation. Omitted
49		// from JSON when empty so dispatches that pass no tuning don't carry a
50		// "params: {}" placeholder.
51		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
52	}
53

140	// SubagentConfig declares per-Project provider+model selection (Phase 3 D-C2).
141	//
142	// Resolution chain at dispatch time (in each reconciler before calling
143	// Dispatcher.Run): Spec.Subagent.Levels.{level}.{image,model} → Spec.Subagent.{image,model}
144	// → Helm-chart default. Vendor + model are orthogonal axes — Image picks the
145	// vendor (via the container image's bundled Subagent impl); Model picks the
146	// model identifier passed to that vendor's CLI/SDK.
147	type SubagentConfig struct {
148		// Image is the default subagent image reference for all levels.
149		// Example: "ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:v0.1.0".
150		// +optional
151		Image string `json:"image,omitempty"`
152	
153		// Model is the default model identifier for all levels.
154		// Example: "claude-sonnet-4-6" (anthropic), "o1-mini" (openai).
155		// +optional
156		Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
157	
158		// Levels carries per-level overrides; any subset is valid.
159		// +optional
160		Levels LevelOverrides `json:"levels,omitempty"`
161	}
162	
163	// LevelOverrides carries per-level provider/model overrides (Phase 3 D-C2).
164	// Each pointer is nil when no override is set for that level.
165	type LevelOverrides struct {
166		// Milestone optionally overrides settings for the milestone planner.
167		// +optional
168		Milestone *LevelConfig `json:"milestone,omitempty"`
169	
170		// Phase optionally overrides settings for the phase planner.
171		// +optional
172		Phase *LevelConfig `json:"phase,omitempty"`
173	
174		// Plan optionally overrides settings for the plan planner.
175		// +optional
176		Plan *LevelConfig `json:"plan,omitempty"`
177	
178		// Task optionally overrides settings for the task executor.
179		// +optional
180		Task *LevelConfig `json:"task,omitempty"`
181	}
182	
183	// LevelConfig declares the per-level subagent override (Phase 3 D-C2).
184	type LevelConfig struct {
185		// Model identifier for this level (e.g., "claude-haiku-4-5").
186		// +optional
187		Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
188	
189		// Params carries per-vendor tuning passthrough (temperature, thinking
190		// budget, etc.). Validation lives in the provider's Subagent impl.
191		// +optional
192		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
193	
194		// Image override is schema-present-but-not-enforced in v1.0 (deferred
195		// to v1.x per CONTEXT.md "Deferred Ideas" — per-level cross-vendor
196		// image overrides). Set this in v1.0 only if the consumer is prepared
197		// to ignore it.
198		// +optional
199		Image string `json:"image,omitempty"`
200	}
201	
202	// GitConfig declares the per-Project git target + creds (Phase 3 D-B6).
203	// HTTPS+PAT is the primary v1.0 path; SSH is documented with host-key caveats
204	// per REQUIREMENTS.md ART-05 but defaults to HTTPS+PAT.
205	type GitConfig struct {
206		// RepoURL is the URL of the target git remote. Supports http:// and https://
207		// (pure-Go go-git transport, production default) and SSH
208		// (git@host:owner/repo.git, documented with host-key caveats).
209		// file:// is NOT supported: go-git's file:// transport shells out to a
210		// system git binary absent from production images.
211		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^(https?://|git@).+`
212		RepoURL string `json:"repoURL"`
213	
214		// CredsSecretRef is the K8s Secret name (same-namespace) carrying the
215		// HTTPS PAT in data key GIT_PAT. Cross-namespace Secret refs are NOT
216		// permitted in v1.0 (threat-mitigation deferred to push Job — plan 03-06).
217		CredsSecretRef string `json:"credsSecretRef"`
218	
219		// LeaksConfigRef is an optional ConfigMap name carrying gitleaks rule

16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	import "context"
20	
21	// Subagent is the out-of-tree contract for TIDE subagent image runtimes
22	// (D-A1 / REQ-SUB-01). Out-of-tree image authors implement this interface to
23	// integrate a custom LLM backend into TIDE without modifying the orchestrator.
24	//
25	// In-repo reference implementations:
26	//   - cmd/stub-subagent — canned test responses, no LLM call (Plan 04).
27	//   - internal/harness  — cap enforcement + signed-token client + secret
28	//     redaction + output-path validation (Plan 06). The harness wraps a
29	//     concrete Subagent implementation selected at startup.
30	//
31	// The concrete Anthropic-backed implementation (Phase 3) satisfies this
32	// interface behind the provider firewall enforced by `make verify-dispatch-imports`.
33	type Subagent interface {
34		// Run executes the task described by in and returns the result envelope.
35		// The context deadline should be set to caps.wallClockSeconds + grace; the
36		// harness also enforces the cap internally via SIGTERM (HARN-02).
37		//
38		// A non-nil error indicates a dispatch-level failure (network, I/O);
39		// task-level failures (subagent exited non-zero) are expressed via
40		// EnvelopeOut.ExitCode and EnvelopeOut.Reason.
41		Run(ctx context.Context, in EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
42	}
43

2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	import (
20		"encoding/json"
21		"fmt"
22		"strings"
23	)
24	
25	// PriceOverride is a provider-agnostic per-model price correction in US cents
26	// per million tokens. Zero cache fields derive from input (read=0.10x, write=1.25x).
27	//
28	// This type lives in pkg/dispatch (the provider-agnostic dispatch contract package,
29	// already imported by both cmd/manager and internal/subagent/anthropic) so the
30	// manager can validate the JSON at startup (Plan 14-05) WITHOUT importing the
31	// anthropic package — the provider firewall stays intact (D-C1).
32	type PriceOverride struct {
33		InputCentsPerMTok      int64 `json:"inputCentsPerMTok"`
34		OutputCentsPerMTok     int64 `json:"outputCentsPerMTok"`
35		CacheReadCentsPerMTok  int64 `json:"cacheReadCentsPerMTok,omitempty"`
36		CacheWriteCentsPerMTok int64 `json:"cacheWriteCentsPerMTok,omitempty"`
37	}
38	
39	// ParsePricingOverrides unmarshals s (empty or "{}" → empty map, nil error) and
40	// validates: Input and Output MUST be > 0; cache fields MUST be >= 0.
41	//
42	// Validation per ASVS V5 / threat T-14-01: reject zero or negative
43	// InputCentsPerMTok/OutputCentsPerMTok and negative cache fields, with the model
44	// ID in the error string so the operator's fix is mechanical. A bad override must
45	// not fail the session (the manager validates at startup in Plan 14-05; the subagent
46	// performs defense-in-depth validation here).
47	func ParsePricingOverrides(s string) (map[string]PriceOverride, error) {
48		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
49		if s == "" || s == "{}" {
50			return map[string]PriceOverride{}, nil
51		}
52	
53		var raw map[string]PriceOverride
54		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(s), &raw); err != nil {
55			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pricing overrides: invalid JSON: %w", err)
56		}
57	
58		for modelID, p := range raw {
59			if p.InputCentsPerMTok <= 0 {
60				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
61					"pricing overrides: model %q has invalid inputCentsPerMTok %d (must be > 0)",
62					modelID, p.InputCentsPerMTok)
63			}
64			if p.OutputCentsPerMTok <= 0 {
65				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
66					"pricing overrides: model %q has invalid outputCentsPerMTok %d (must be > 0)",
67					modelID, p.OutputCentsPerMTok)
68			}
69			if p.CacheReadCentsPerMTok < 0 {
70				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
71					"pricing overrides: model %q has negative cacheReadCentsPerMTok %d (must be >= 0)",
72					modelID, p.CacheReadCentsPerMTok)
73			}
74			if p.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok < 0 {
75				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
76					"pricing overrides: model %q has negative cacheWriteCentsPerMTok %d (must be >= 0)",
77					modelID, p.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok)
78			}
79		}
80	
81		return raw, nil
82	}
83

1	# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
2	# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
3	# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
4	#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
5	#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
6	#
7	# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
8	FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
9	ARG TARGETOS
10	ARG TARGETARCH
11	
12	WORKDIR /workspace
13	
14	# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
15	COPY go.mod go.sum ./
16	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download
17	
18	# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
19	COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
20	COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
21	COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
22	COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
23	COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/
24	
25	# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
26	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
27	    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
28	      -ldflags="-s -w" \
29	      -o /out/claude-subagent \
30	      ./cmd/claude-subagent
31	
32	# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
33	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
34	# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
35	# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
36	# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
37	# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
38	# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
39	#
40	# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
41	# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
42	# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
43	# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
44	# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
45	# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
46	FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732
47	
48	RUN apt-get update \
49	    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
50	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
51	    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
52	    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
53	    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
54	    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
55	    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
56	    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
57	    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'
58	
59	RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142
60	
61	COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent
62	
63	# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
64	USER 1000
65	
66	ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]
67

file.content:
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	// Command claude-subagent — Phase 3 real-Claude swap of stub-subagent. Thin
18	// shim: load EnvelopeIn → anthropic.Run → write EnvelopeOut. Ignores the
19	// envelope's Dev struct (PATTERNS.md line 442 / D-F1).
20	package main
21	
22	import (
23		"context"
24		"encoding/json"
25		"flag"
26		"fmt"
27		"io"
28		"os"
29		"os/signal"
30		"path/filepath"
31		"syscall"
32		"time"
33	
34		"github.com/go-git/go-git/v5/plumbing"
35		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/harness"
36		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/anthropic"
37		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
38	)
39	
40	// parsePricingOverridesFromEnv reads TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON and parses it.
41	// An empty or missing env var returns a nil map (no overrides). An invalid JSON
42	// value logs a loud stderr warning and returns nil (bad override must not fail
43	// the session — Plan 14-05 validates at manager startup; this is defense in depth).
44	func parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr io.Writer) map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride {
45		raw := os.Getenv("TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON")
46		if raw == "" {
47			return nil
48		}
49		overrides, err := pkgdispatch.ParsePricingOverrides(raw)
50		if err != nil {
51			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "pricing: invalid TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON, ignoring overrides: %v\n", err)
52			return nil
53		}
54		return overrides
55	}
56	
57	type anthropicRunner interface {
58		Run(ctx context.Context, in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error)
59	}
60	
61	var newSubagent = func(
62		claudeBinary, workspaceRoot string,
63		pricingOverrides map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride,
64	) anthropicRunner {
65		return anthropic.New(anthropic.Options{
66			ClaudeBinary:     claudeBinary,
67			WorkspaceRoot:    workspaceRoot,
68			PricingOverrides: pricingOverrides,
69		})
70	}
71	var ensureWorktreeFunc = harness.EnsureWorktree
72	var commitWorktreeFunc = func(worktreeDir, taskUID string) (plumbing.Hash, bool, error) {
73		return harness.CommitWorktree(worktreeDir, taskUID)
74	}
75	
76	func main() {
77		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("claude-subagent", flag.ExitOnError)
78		envelopePath := fs.String("envelope-path", "",
79			"path to EnvelopeIn JSON (default: $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH or /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json)")
80		workspaceRoot := fs.String("workspace-root", "/workspace", "PVC mount root")
81		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[1:]); err != nil {
82			os.Exit(2)
83		}
84		// Resolve envelope path: flag → $TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH → per-task-uid default.
85		// This MUST match stub-subagent (cmd/stub-subagent/main.go) and the
86		// controller's write location, which is /workspace/envelopes/$TIDE_TASK_UID/in.json
87		// (the controller passes no --envelope-path arg, so it relies on this default).
88		// The previous hardcoded /workspace/envelopes/in.json default did not include
89		// the per-task-uid subdirectory, so the real claude planner/executor could never
90		// find its input envelope. Only surfaced in live real-Claude runs — CI exercises
91		// the stub, whose default already matched.
92		if *envelopePath == "" {
93			if p := os.Getenv("TIDE_ENVELOPE_PATH"); p != "" {
94				*envelopePath = p
95			} else {
96				*envelopePath = fmt.Sprintf("/workspace/envelopes/%s/in.json", os.Getenv("TIDE_TASK_UID"))
97			}
98		}
99		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGTERM, syscall.SIGINT)
100		defer cancel()
101		os.Exit(run(ctx, *envelopePath, *workspaceRoot, os.Stdout, os.Stderr))
102	}
103	
104	// run is the testable entry point. Does NOT branch on env.Dev.TestMode.
105	//
106	//nolint:unparam // stdout is part of the shared subagent-binary (stdout, stderr) entry-point contract
107	func run(ctx context.Context, envelopePath, workspaceRoot string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) int {
108		outPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(envelopePath), "out.json")
109		env, err := harness.ReadEnvelopeIn(envelopePath)
110		if err != nil {
111			return failOut(stderr, outPath, "", err, 2, "invalid-envelope")
112		}
113		if err := ensureWorktreeFunc(env, workspaceRoot, env.Branch); err != nil {
114			return failOut(stderr, outPath, env.TaskUID, err, 1, "worktree-setup-failed")
115		}
116		// D-02: read pricing overrides from TIDE_PRICING_OVERRIDES_JSON env.
117		// Invalid JSON logs a loud warning and falls back to compiled table (defense in depth).
118		pricingOverrides := parsePricingOverridesFromEnv(stderr)
119		out, runErr := newSubagent("claude", workspaceRoot, pricingOverrides).Run(ctx, env)
120		if runErr != nil {
121			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: %v\n", runErr)
122			out = failEnvelope(env.TaskUID, runErr, 1, "subagent-error")
123		}
124		if runErr == nil && env.Role == "executor" {
125			worktreeDir := filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, "worktrees", env.TaskUID)
126			hash, isEmpty, commitErr := commitWorktreeFunc(worktreeDir, env.TaskUID)
127			if commitErr != nil {
128				out = failEnvelope(env.TaskUID, commitErr, 1, "commit-failed")
129			} else if isEmpty {
130				out.ExitCode = 1
131				out.Result = "empty-diff"
132				out.Reason = "executor produced no changes in worktree"
133			} else {
134				out.Git = &pkgdispatch.GitOutput{HeadSHA: hash.String()}
135			}
136		}
137		if err := writeEnvelope(outPath, out); err != nil {
138			fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: write out.json: %v\n", err)
139			return 2
140		}
141		return out.ExitCode
142	}
143	
144	func failEnvelope(taskUID string, err error, exitCode int, result string) pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut {
145		return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{
146			APIVersion: pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1, Kind: pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeOut,
147			TaskUID: taskUID, ExitCode: exitCode, Result: result, Reason: err.Error(),
148			CompletedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
149		}
150	}
151	
152	func failOut(stderr io.Writer, outPath, taskUID string, err error, exitCode int, result string) int {
153		fmt.Fprintf(stderr, "claude-subagent: %v\n", err)
154		_ = writeEnvelope(outPath, failEnvelope(taskUID, err, exitCode, result))
155		return exitCode
156	}
157	
158	func writeEnvelope(path string, out pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut) error {
159		// Write the full envelope to the PVC (audit artifact + reporter Job input).
160		// This is UNCHANGED — out.json on the PVC carries ChildCRDs + verbose Result.
161		if err := harness.WriteEnvelopeOut(path, out); err != nil {
162			return err
163		}
164		// Write only the tiny TerminationStub to the termination message path.
165		// The stub carries ExitCode/Reason/Usage/HeadSHA and stays <4KB regardless
166		// of ChildCRDs or Result size (#11 root cause fix; T-09-03 mitigation).
167		stub := pkgdispatch.NewTerminationStub(out)
168		data, _ := json.Marshal(stub)
169		tp := os.Getenv("TIDE_TERMINATION_MESSAGE_PATH")
170		if tp == "" {
171			tp = "/dev/termination-log"
172		}
173		_ = os.WriteFile(tp, data, 0o644)
174		return nil
175	}
176
