1	# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
2	#
3	# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
4	# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
5	# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
6	# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
7	# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
8	# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
9	# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
10	#
11	# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
12	# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
13	# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
14	# Phase 5-compatible foundation.
15	
16	# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
17	controllerManager:
18	  manager:
19	    args:
20	    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
21	    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
22	    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
23	    - --leader-elect
24	    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
25	    containerSecurityContext:
26	      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
27	      capabilities:
28	        drop:
29	        - ALL
30	      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
31	    env:
32	      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
33	      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
34	      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
35	      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
36	      watchNamespace: ""
37	    image:
38	      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
39	      tag: ""
40	      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
41	    podAnnotations: {}
42	    resources:
43	      limits:
44	        cpu: 500m
45	        memory: 128Mi
46	      requests:
47	        cpu: 10m
48	        memory: 64Mi
49	  podSecurityContext:
50	    runAsNonRoot: true
51	    seccompProfile:
52	      type: RuntimeDefault
53	  replicas: 1
54	  serviceAccount:
55	    annotations: {}
56	kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
57	metricsService:
58	  ports:
59	  - name: https
60	    port: 8443
61	    protocol: TCP
62	    targetPort: 8443
63	  type: ClusterIP
64	webhookService:
65	  ports:
66	  - port: 443
67	    protocol: TCP
68	    targetPort: 9443
69	  type: ClusterIP
70	
71	# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
72	# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
73	#
74	# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
75	# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
76	# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
77	# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
78	plannerConcurrency: 16
79	executorConcurrency: 4
80	
81	# Per-Kind controller-runtime MaxConcurrentReconciles. Values match the Plan 04
82	# defaults — see internal/config/defaults.go. Project/Milestone are singletons-per-
83	# parent in the common case (1 is enough); Wave/Task fan out wider.
84	maxConcurrentReconciles:
85	  project: 1
86	  milestone: 1
87	  phase: 2
88	  plan: 4
89	  wave: 8
90	  task: 16
91	
92	# CTRL-03 leader election. Enabled by default; the controller's
93	# --leader-elect arg is appended in controllerManager.manager.args above.
94	#
95	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-D1 chaos-resume tuning): leaseDurationSeconds,
96	# renewDeadlineSeconds, and retryPeriodSeconds flow through to
97	# controller-runtime via TIDE_LEADER_{LEASE,RENEW,RETRY}_SECONDS env vars on
98	# the manager Deployment. Defaults give a single-pod restart ≤25s failover
99	# ceiling. Invariant: lease > renew > retry (controller-runtime validates).
100	leaderElection:
101	  enabled: true
102	  leaseDurationSeconds: 15
103	  renewDeadlineSeconds: 10
104	  retryPeriodSeconds: 2
105	
106	# Phase 2 additions (Plan 12 wiring — D-C3, D-D1, D-D3, Blocker #1/#2/#3 fixes).
107	
108	# Plan admission file-touch validation mode (D-E3 / Plan 11 PlanCustomValidator).
109	# "warn" is the safe Helm default — operators opt into "strict" via Project.Spec or
110	# --set planAdmission.fileTouchMode=strict. Passed to the controller via
111	# --default-file-touch-mode flag in the Deployment args.
112	planAdmission:
113	  fileTouchMode: warn
114	
115	# Default rate-limit parameters for provider credential Secrets (D-D3).
116	# Applied when no Secret annotation or Project.Spec.Providers entry overrides them.
117	# Passed to the controller via --rate-limit-default-rpm and --rate-limit-default-burst flags.
118	rateLimits:
119	  defaults:
120	    requestsPerMinute: 60
121	    tokensPerMinute: 100000  # informational v1 — not yet enforced at the bucket level
122	    burst: 10
123	
124	# Pricing overrides (D-02): per-model price corrections merged OVER the compiled
125	# priceTable at controller startup. Operators use this to correct price drift without
126	# a TIDE release. Keys are exact model ID strings; values are cents per million tokens.
127	# Passed to the controller via --pricing-overrides-json flag (JSON-encoded map).
128	#
129	# Example (all fields optional — omitted fields inherit compiled defaults):
130	#   pricing:
131	#     overrides:
132	#       claude-some-future-model:
133	#         inputCentsPerMTok: 300
134	#         outputCentsPerMTok: 1500
135	#         cacheReadCentsPerMTok: 30
136	#         cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 375
137	pricing:
138	  overrides: {}
139	
140	# Phase 2 image refs for credproxy sidecar + build/load tooling (D-A2).
141	# images.stubSubagent.* keys are retained for image build/load tooling
142	# (make test-int-kind-prep loads the stub image by tag), but are NO LONGER
143	# injected as a --subagent-image flag into the Deployment args (Phase 13 D-01;
144	# that flag was silently forcing the stub in every v1.0.0 install and has been
145	# dropped). Subagent image default now flows via subagent.defaults.image →
146	# CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
147	#
148	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 additions: claudeSubagent (D-C2 real-Claude image, swaps
149	# in alongside stubSubagent; both images stay buildable so chaos-resume and
150	# kind smoke tests keep working with the stub) and tidePush (D-B1 dedicated
151	# push-Job image — orchestrator-side gitleaks + go-git push-with-lease).
152	#
153	# Production reproducibility: tags are mutable in the OCI spec; pin by
154	# `@sha256:<digest>` in production overrides (`--set images.tidePush.tag=...`
155	# with the digest, or set repository: ghcr.io/.../tide-push@sha256:...).
156	images:
157	  stubSubagent:
158	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent
159	    tag: ""
160	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
161	  credProxy:
162	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-credproxy
163	    tag: ""
164	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
165	  busybox:
166	    repository: busybox
167	    tag: "1.36"
168	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
169	  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B1): dedicated tide-push image. Orchestrator-side
170	  # push Job per level boundary; gitleaks + go-git + --force-with-lease.
171	  # Wired to controller via TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
172	  tidePush:
173	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-push
174	    tag: ""
175	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
176	  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C2 swap-in): real Claude-Code-backed subagent image.
177	  # Used as the default fallback when Project.Spec.subagent.image is empty;
178	  # individual Projects can pin a different vendor image per Project (D-C2).
179	  # Wired to controller via CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
180	  # In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest — tag-based pulls of mutable tags
181	  # are not reproducible across pulls.
182	  claudeSubagent:
183	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
184	    tag: ""
185	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
186	  # Phase 09 plan 09-06 (REQ-09-01): in-namespace reporter image. The reader Job
187	  # runs in the project namespace as tide-reporter SA, reads out.json from the PVC,
188	  # and materializes child CRDs via the K8s API (Option C architecture).
189	  # Wired to controller via TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
190	  # When empty, the spawn site skips Job creation (same as TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE skip in
191	  # boundary_push.go). In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest.
192	  tideReporter:
193	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-reporter
194	    tag: ""
195	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
196	
197	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C4): per-level subagent defaults. Resolution chain at
198	# dispatch time (in MilestoneReconciler / PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler before
199	# calling Dispatcher.Run):
200	#
201	#   Levels.<level>.Image (project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.image) ← per-level override
202	#   → Spec.Subagent.Image (project.Spec.subagent.image)               ← per-Project default
203	#   → subagent.defaults.image (this value → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env) ← Helm chart default
204	#
205	#   project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.model  ← per-level model override
206	#   project.Spec.subagent.model                 ← per-Project model default
207	#   subagent.defaults.model                     ← Helm chart default (this block)
208	#   subagent.levels.<level>.model               ← Helm per-level default (this block)
209	#
210	# Phase 13 D-01 image posture: production default is the real Claude-backed subagent
211	# (ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent). The stub is explicit opt-in for tests/CI
212	# only — it is never injected implicitly. To use the stub:
213	#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
214	# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
215	# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
216	# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
217	#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
218	#
219	# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
220	#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
221	#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
222	#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
223	#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
224	#
225	# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
226	# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
227	subagent:
228	  defaults:
229	    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
230	    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
231	  levels:
232	    milestone:
233	      model: claude-opus-4-7
234	    phase:
235	      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
236	    plan:
237	      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
238	    task:
239	      model: claude-haiku-4-5
240	
241	# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
242	# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
243	# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
244	# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
245	# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
246	gitleaks:
247	  configMapName: ""
248	
249	# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
250	# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
251	# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
252	# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
253	# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
254	# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
255	# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
256	signingKey:
257	  secretName: tide-signing-key
258	
259	# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
260	# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
261	budget:
262	  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
263	  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
264	  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
265	  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
266	  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
267	  defaults:
268	    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
269	    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default
270	
271	# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
272	#
273	# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
274	# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
275	# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
276	# any pre-Phase-4 keys.
277	
278	# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
279	# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
280	# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
281	# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
282	# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
283	#
284	# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
285	# operators who want the controller-only install set
286	# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
287	dashboard:
288	  enabled: true
289	  image:
290	    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
291	    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
292	    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
293	  replicas: 1
294	  resources:
295	    requests:
296	      cpu: 50m
297	      memory: 64Mi
298	    limits:
299	      cpu: 200m
300	      memory: 256Mi
301	  service:
302	    type: ClusterIP
303	    port: 80
304	    targetPort: 8080
305	
306	# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
307	#
308	# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
309	#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
310	#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
311	#
312	# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
313	# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
314	prometheus:
315	  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
316	  #
317	  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
318	  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
319	  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
320	  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
321	  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
322	  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
323	  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
324	  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
325	  #
326	  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
327	  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to
328	  # live-only CRD-.status views (no error state, no blank panel, no broken
329	  # layout). Set e.g.
330	  #   --set prometheus.endpoint=http://prometheus-operated.monitoring:9090
331	  # to wire historical cost-over-time charts.
332	  endpoint: ""
333	  # Prometheus local-TSDB retention window for telemetry history (Phase 4 -- Q3).
334	  #
335	  # This chart ships a ServiceMonitor only (serviceMonitor block below) and does
336	  # NOT bundle a Prometheus server, so this value configures no in-chart
337	  # component. It is the single documented knob for the retention operators must
338	  # apply as --storage.tsdb.retention.time=<value> on their operator-managed
339	  # Prometheus.
340	  #
341	  # RETENTION MATH: one multi-day dogfood run plus a one-week post-run analysis
342	  # window fits comfortably inside the 15d default. For organizations tracking
343	  # cost-over-time trends ACROSS multiple runs (weeks of continuous dogfooding),
344	  # set "30d" or longer so the cost-over-time charts span the full cross-run
345	  # history.
346	  retentionTime: "15d"
347	  serviceMonitor:
348	    enabled: false
349	    interval: 30s
350	    scrapeTimeout: 10s
351	
352	# OpenTelemetry env-driven config (Phase 4 D-O3 / plan 04-03).
353	#
354	# Read by internal/otelinit at controller boot:
355	#   - exporter.endpoint = ""  → no-op TracerProvider (zero overhead; default)
356	#   - exporter.endpoint = "otel-collector:4317" → OTLP gRPC exporter
357	#
358	# Sampler is env-driven (NOT WithSampler in code, per Pitfall 24 mitigation):
359	# OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER + OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG. Defaults trace 10% of root
360	# spans (`parentbased_traceidratio` arg `0.1`).
361	otel:
362	  exporter:
363	    endpoint: ""
364	  tracesSampler: "parentbased_traceidratio"
365	  tracesSamplerArg: "0.1"
366	  serviceName: "tide-controller-manager"
367	
368	# Single shared RWX PVC for all Projects' workspaces (Blocker #2/#3 fix —
369	# RESEARCH.md Open Question #2 RESOLVED with the single-shared-PVC + subPath architecture).
370	#
371	# Architecture: ONE chart-provisioned tide-projects PVC (ReadWriteMany), Helm-templated at
372	# install time via charts/tide/templates/projects-pvc.yaml.
373	#   - Manager Pod mounts PVC at /workspaces (no subPath) — reads /workspaces/{project-uid}/workspace/...
374	#   - Task Pods mount PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace → appearing as /workspace
375	#   - Init Job (Plan 10) mounts PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace and mkdirs the layout
376	#
377	# Trade-off: single shared PVC means per-Project quota story doesn't fall out of CSI;
378	# quotas are Project.Spec.Budget-driven (FAIL-04) not storage-driven.
379	# Phase 3 ART-02 evaluates per-Project PVCs if/when this becomes a real concern.
380	#
381	# storageClassName: "" uses the cluster-default StorageClass (suitable for kind/minikube/dev).
382	# For production, set to a CSI driver that supports ReadWriteMany (e.g. nfs-csi, ceph-cephfs).
383	workspaces:
384	  pvc:
385	    create: true
386	    name: tide-projects
387	    size: 10Gi
388	    storageClassName: ""  # cluster default; set to a CSI RWX driver for production
389	    accessModes: [ReadWriteMany]  # production-default; matches the chart's single-shared-RWX-PVC architecture (see comment above). Override via `--set workspaces.pvc.accessModes={RWO-mode}` for single-node kind/minikube test clusters whose default provisioner (rancher.io/local-path) only supports the non-shared access modes.
390	
391	# Phase 5 D-X4 — per-namespace RoleBinding template (AUTH-02 catch-up).
392	# Empty default: no per-namespace RoleBindings shipped unless operator opts in.
393	# For multi-Project installs, list the Project namespaces here:
394	#   projectNamespaces:
395	#     - tide-customer-acme
396	#     - tide-customer-globex
397	projectNamespaces: []
398

file.content:
# TIDE controller chart values (Phase 1 surface — D-E1).
#
# This file is the canonical, hand-maintained values.yaml for charts/tide/. The
# `make helm-controller` target generates charts/tide/ via helmify, then copies
# THIS file over the helmify-emitted charts/tide/values.yaml. Helmify cannot
# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 9443
  type: ClusterIP

# Phase 1 runtime tunables (D-E1) — rendered into the tide-config ConfigMap which is
# mounted at /etc/tide/config.yaml and parsed by internal/config.Load (CTRL-04 / Plan 04).
#
# Two distinct parallelism budgets per Pitfall 6: planning fans out wide (Milestone/
# Phase/Plan reconciles), execution fans out narrow (Wave/Task reconciles). The
# crosspool analyzer (cmd/tide-lint) prevents the two budgets from being unified at
# compile time; this values surface keeps them separately tunable at deploy time.
plannerConcurrency: 16
executorConcurrency: 4

# Per-Kind controller-runtime MaxConcurrentReconciles. Values match the Plan 04
# defaults — see internal/config/defaults.go. Project/Milestone are singletons-per-
# parent in the common case (1 is enough); Wave/Task fan out wider.
maxConcurrentReconciles:
  project: 1
  milestone: 1
  phase: 2
  plan: 4
  wave: 8
  task: 16

# CTRL-03 leader election. Enabled by default; the controller's
# --leader-elect arg is appended in controllerManager.manager.args above.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-D1 chaos-resume tuning): leaseDurationSeconds,
# renewDeadlineSeconds, and retryPeriodSeconds flow through to
# controller-runtime via TIDE_LEADER_{LEASE,RENEW,RETRY}_SECONDS env vars on
# the manager Deployment. Defaults give a single-pod restart ≤25s failover
# ceiling. Invariant: lease > renew > retry (controller-runtime validates).
leaderElection:
  enabled: true
  leaseDurationSeconds: 15
  renewDeadlineSeconds: 10
  retryPeriodSeconds: 2

# Phase 2 additions (Plan 12 wiring — D-C3, D-D1, D-D3, Blocker #1/#2/#3 fixes).

# Plan admission file-touch validation mode (D-E3 / Plan 11 PlanCustomValidator).
# "warn" is the safe Helm default — operators opt into "strict" via Project.Spec or
# --set planAdmission.fileTouchMode=strict. Passed to the controller via
# --default-file-touch-mode flag in the Deployment args.
planAdmission:
  fileTouchMode: warn

# Default rate-limit parameters for provider credential Secrets (D-D3).
# Applied when no Secret annotation or Project.Spec.Providers entry overrides them.
# Passed to the controller via --rate-limit-default-rpm and --rate-limit-default-burst flags.
rateLimits:
  defaults:
    requestsPerMinute: 60
    tokensPerMinute: 100000  # informational v1 — not yet enforced at the bucket level
    burst: 10

# Pricing overrides (D-02): per-model price corrections merged OVER the compiled
# priceTable at controller startup. Operators use this to correct price drift without
# a TIDE release. Keys are exact model ID strings; values are cents per million tokens.
# Passed to the controller via --pricing-overrides-json flag (JSON-encoded map).
#
# Example (all fields optional — omitted fields inherit compiled defaults):
#   pricing:
#     overrides:
#       claude-some-future-model:
#         inputCentsPerMTok: 300
#         outputCentsPerMTok: 1500
#         cacheReadCentsPerMTok: 30
#         cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 375
pricing:
  overrides: {}

# Phase 2 image refs for credproxy sidecar + build/load tooling (D-A2).
# images.stubSubagent.* keys are retained for image build/load tooling
# (make test-int-kind-prep loads the stub image by tag), but are NO LONGER
# injected as a --subagent-image flag into the Deployment args (Phase 13 D-01;
# that flag was silently forcing the stub in every v1.0.0 install and has been
# dropped). Subagent image default now flows via subagent.defaults.image →
# CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
#
# Phase 3 plan 03-09 additions: claudeSubagent (D-C2 real-Claude image, swaps
# in alongside stubSubagent; both images stay buildable so chaos-resume and
# kind smoke tests keep working with the stub) and tidePush (D-B1 dedicated
# push-Job image — orchestrator-side gitleaks + go-git push-with-lease).
#
# Production reproducibility: tags are mutable in the OCI spec; pin by
# `@sha256:<digest>` in production overrides (`--set images.tidePush.tag=...`
# with the digest, or set repository: ghcr.io/.../tide-push@sha256:...).
images:
  stubSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  credProxy:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-credproxy
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  busybox:
    repository: busybox
    tag: "1.36"
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B1): dedicated tide-push image. Orchestrator-side
  # push Job per level boundary; gitleaks + go-git + --force-with-lease.
  # Wired to controller via TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
  tidePush:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-push
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C2 swap-in): real Claude-Code-backed subagent image.
  # Used as the default fallback when Project.Spec.subagent.image is empty;
  # individual Projects can pin a different vendor image per Project (D-C2).
  # Wired to controller via CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var on manager Deployment.
  # In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest — tag-based pulls of mutable tags
  # are not reproducible across pulls.
  claudeSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Phase 09 plan 09-06 (REQ-09-01): in-namespace reporter image. The reader Job
  # runs in the project namespace as tide-reporter SA, reads out.json from the PVC,
  # and materializes child CRDs via the K8s API (Option C architecture).
  # Wired to controller via TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE env var on the manager Deployment.
  # When empty, the spawn site skips Job creation (same as TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE skip in
  # boundary_push.go). In production, prefer pinning by @sha256 digest.
  tideReporter:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-reporter
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-C4): per-level subagent defaults. Resolution chain at
# dispatch time (in MilestoneReconciler / PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler before
# calling Dispatcher.Run):
#
#   Levels.<level>.Image (project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.image) ← per-level override
#   → Spec.Subagent.Image (project.Spec.subagent.image)               ← per-Project default
#   → subagent.defaults.image (this value → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env) ← Helm chart default
#
#   project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.model  ← per-level model override
#   project.Spec.subagent.model                 ← per-Project model default
#   subagent.defaults.model                     ← Helm chart default (this block)
#   subagent.levels.<level>.model               ← Helm per-level default (this block)
#
# Phase 13 D-01 image posture: production default is the real Claude-backed subagent
# (ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent). The stub is explicit opt-in for tests/CI
# only — it is never injected implicitly. To use the stub:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
# Bare refs (no ":<tag>" or "@sha256:" suffix) have ":<appVersion>" appended by the
# chart template. Tag- or digest-qualified values pass through verbatim.
# Digest pinning is recommended for production reproducibility:
#   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent@sha256:<digest>
#
# Per-level model defaults (D-C4) match the phase-brief lock-in:
#   milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
#   phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
#   task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
#
# Per-level models flow through the manager Deployment env block as
# TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_{MILESTONE,PHASE,PLAN,TASK}.
subagent:
  defaults:
    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent
    model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  levels:
    milestone:
      model: claude-opus-4-7
    phase:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    plan:
      model: claude-sonnet-4-6
    task:
      model: claude-haiku-4-5

# Phase 3 plan 03-09 (D-B3): optional cluster-wide gitleaks override.
# Empty (default) → push Job uses the compiled-in gitleaks v8 ruleset.
# Per-Project overrides are still supported via Project.Spec.git.leaksConfigRef
# (D-B3 — ConfigMap mounted into push Job at /etc/tide/gitleaks-config.toml).
# This top-level key is the cluster-wide fallback (v1.0 deferred; reserved for v1.x).
gitleaks:
  configMapName: ""

# HMAC signing-secret name (D-C3 / Blocker #1 fix).
# The signing-secret.yaml Helm template creates a Secret with this name on first install
# using Helm's `lookup` + `if not` guard (idempotent). resource-policy: keep annotation
# preserves the Secret across helm upgrade/uninstall so in-flight tokens stay valid
# (T-02-12-02 / T-02-12-03 mitigations).
# The Secret's data key is TIDE_SIGNING_KEY (env-friendly) so envFrom: [{secretRef: ...}]
# auto-populates the TIDE_SIGNING_KEY env var on the Manager Deployment and credproxy sidecar.
signingKey:
  secretName: tide-signing-key

# Budget defaults (D-D2 / FAIL-04). Helm-driven hard caps applied to all Projects that
# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 256Mi
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
    targetPort: 8080

# Prometheus ServiceMonitor (Phase 4 D-O6).
#
# DEFAULT OFF per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern:
#   "Default the chart's ServiceMonitor to prometheus.enabled=false to avoid
#    CRD-not-found on plain clusters."
#
# Enable explicitly via `--set prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled=true` once
# the prometheus-operator CRDs (monitoring.coreos.com/v1) are installed.
prometheus:
  # PromQL-proxy endpoint (Phase 4 telemetry surface -- milestone-01 Q1 lock-in).
  #
  # Q1 DECISION: PROXY, not a second browser-side datasource. This is a
  # SERVER-SIDE value. When non-empty it is injected as the PROM_ENDPOINT env
  # var into the dashboard container (templates/dashboard-deployment.yaml, landed
  # by plan-02), and the dashboard chi server proxies PromQL via
  # GET /api/v1/query and GET /api/v1/query_range. The browser therefore never
  # learns a second network target: single origin, no CORS, one auth surface,
  # and the Prometheus URL stays confined to the dashboard process (matters when
  # Prometheus is ClusterIP-only and unreachable from outside the cluster).
  #
  # Empty (default) => graceful degradation: the proxy returns HTTP 200 with the
  # body {"status":"unavailable"} and the React Telemetry tab falls back to
  # live-only CRD-.status views (no error state, no blank panel, no broken
  # layout). Set e.g.
  #   --set prometheus.endpoint=http://prometheus-operated.monitoring:9090
  # to wire historical cost-over-time charts.
  endpoint: ""
  # Prometheus local-TSDB retention window for telemetry history (Phase 4 -- Q3).
  #
  # This chart ships a ServiceMonitor only (serviceMonitor block below) and does
  # NOT bundle a Prometheus server, so this value configures no in-chart
  # component. It is the single documented knob for the retention operators must
  # apply as --storage.tsdb.retention.time=<value> on their operator-managed
  # Prometheus.
  #
  # RETENTION MATH: one multi-day dogfood run plus a one-week post-run analysis
  # window fits comfortably inside the 15d default. For organizations tracking
  # cost-over-time trends ACROSS multiple runs (weeks of continuous dogfooding),
  # set "30d" or longer so the cost-over-time charts span the full cross-run
  # history.
  retentionTime: "15d"
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: false
    interval: 30s
    scrapeTimeout: 10s

# OpenTelemetry env-driven config (Phase 4 D-O3 / plan 04-03).
#
# Read by internal/otelinit at controller boot:
#   - exporter.endpoint = ""  → no-op TracerProvider (zero overhead; default)
#   - exporter.endpoint = "otel-collector:4317" → OTLP gRPC exporter
#
# Sampler is env-driven (NOT WithSampler in code, per Pitfall 24 mitigation):
# OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER + OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG. Defaults trace 10% of root
# spans (`parentbased_traceidratio` arg `0.1`).
otel:
  exporter:
    endpoint: ""
  tracesSampler: "parentbased_traceidratio"
  tracesSamplerArg: "0.1"
  serviceName: "tide-controller-manager"

# Single shared RWX PVC for all Projects' workspaces (Blocker #2/#3 fix —
# RESEARCH.md Open Question #2 RESOLVED with the single-shared-PVC + subPath architecture).
#
# Architecture: ONE chart-provisioned tide-projects PVC (ReadWriteMany), Helm-templated at
# install time via charts/tide/templates/projects-pvc.yaml.
#   - Manager Pod mounts PVC at /workspaces (no subPath) — reads /workspaces/{project-uid}/workspace/...
#   - Task Pods mount PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace → appearing as /workspace
#   - Init Job (Plan 10) mounts PVC with subPath: {project-uid}/workspace and mkdirs the layout
#
# Trade-off: single shared PVC means per-Project quota story doesn't fall out of CSI;
# quotas are Project.Spec.Budget-driven (FAIL-04) not storage-driven.
# Phase 3 ART-02 evaluates per-Project PVCs if/when this becomes a real concern.
#
# storageClassName: "" uses the cluster-default StorageClass (suitable for kind/minikube/dev).
# For production, set to a CSI driver that supports ReadWriteMany (e.g. nfs-csi, ceph-cephfs).
workspaces:
  pvc:
    create: true
    name: tide-projects
    size: 10Gi
    storageClassName: ""  # cluster default; set to a CSI RWX driver for production
    accessModes: [ReadWriteMany]  # production-default; matches the chart's single-shared-RWX-PVC architecture (see comment above). Override via `--set workspaces.pvc.accessModes={RWO-mode}` for single-node kind/minikube test clusters whose default provisioner (rancher.io/local-path) only supports the non-shared access modes.

# Phase 5 D-X4 — per-namespace RoleBinding template (AUTH-02 catch-up).
# Empty default: no per-namespace RoleBindings shipped unless operator opts in.
# For multi-Project installs, list the Project namespaces here:
#   projectNamespaces:
#     - tide-customer-acme
#     - tide-customer-globex
projectNamespaces: []

file.content:
- /manager
        env:
        - name: WATCH_NAMESPACE
          value: {{ quote .Values.controllerManager.manager.env.watchNamespace }}
        - name: KUBERNETES_CLUSTER_DOMAIN
          value: {{ quote .Values.kubernetesClusterDomain }}
        - name: TIDE_PUSH_IMAGE
          value: "{{ .Values.images.tidePush.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tidePush.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        # Phase 13 D-01: subagent image default channel.
        # Resolution chain (highest priority wins):
        #   Levels.<level>.Image (Project CRD per-level override)
        #   → Spec.Subagent.Image (Project CRD per-project default)
        #   → CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE (this env — Helm chart default)
        #
        # The startup flag injecting the stub image has been removed (Phase 13
        # D-01 deliberate fixed-contract exception). This env is now the sole
        # default tier.
        #
        # Stub opt-in for tests/CI:
        #   --set subagent.defaults.image=ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:<tag>
        # Tag- or digest-qualified values (containing ":" after last "/" or "@sha256:")
        # pass through verbatim. Bare refs get ":<appVersion>" appended automatically.
        # Digest pinning passes through unchanged — recommended for production.
        - name: CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE
          {{- $img := required "subagent.defaults.image must be a non-empty image ref (e.g. ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent:latest)" .Values.subagent.defaults.image }}
          {{- if or (regexMatch ":[^/]+$" $img) (contains "@" $img) }}
          value: {{ $img | quote }}
          {{- else }}
          value: {{ printf "%s:%s" $img .Chart.AppVersion | quote }}
          {{- end }}
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.milestone.model | default "claude-opus-4-7" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.phase.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.plan.model | default "claude-sonnet-4-6" }}"
        - name: TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
          value: "{{ .Values.subagent.levels.task.model | default "claude-haiku-4-5" }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.leaseDurationSeconds | default 15 }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.renewDeadlineSeconds | default 10 }}"
        - name: TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS
          value: "{{ .Values.leaderElection.retryPeriodSeconds | default 2 }}"
        # phase3-env-injected
        # Phase 4 plan 04-14 (D-O3): OTel env vars read by internal/otelinit.
        # Empty OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT → no-op TracerProvider (zero
        # overhead, default posture for plain clusters). Sampler is env-driven
        # to honor Pitfall 24 mitigation (no WithSampler in code).
        - name: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.exporter.endpoint }}
        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSampler }}
        - name: OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.tracesSamplerArg }}
        - name: OTEL_SERVICE_NAME
          value: {{ quote .Values.otel.serviceName }}
        # phase4-env-injected
        - name: TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE
          value: "{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.repository }}:{{ .Values.images.tideReporter.tag | default .Chart.AppVersion }}"
        # phase9-reporter-env-injected

        envFrom:
        - secretRef:
            name: {{ .Values.signingKey.secretName | default "tide-signing-key" }}
        image: {{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.controllerManager.manager.image.tag
          | default .Chart.AppVersion }}
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 8081
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 20
        name: manager
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: metrics
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8081
          name: health

1	/*
2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	// Env-variable helpers for Helm-driven configuration (Phase 3 plan 03-09).
18	//
19	// These helpers exist as testable units (cmd/manager/env_test.go) because
20	// the main() function itself is not unit-testable (it constructs the
21	// controller-runtime Manager and blocks on mgr.Start). Helpers cover the
22	// per-level model defaults (D-C4) and leader-election tuning (D-D1).
23	package main
24	
25	import (
26		"os"
27		"strconv"
28		"time"
29	
30		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/controller"
31	)
32	
33	// envOrDefault returns the value of the env var named key, or fallback when
34	// the env var is unset or empty. Empty-string is treated as unset so a Helm
35	// value left at its zero default (e.g. `value: ""`) cleanly falls through to
36	// the binary's compile-time default rather than overriding it with "".
37	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
38		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
39			return v
40		}
41		return fallback
42	}
43	
44	// atoiOrDefault returns the integer value of the env var named key, or
45	// fallback when the env var is unset, empty, or not an integer.
46	// Non-integer values are tolerated (return fallback) so a stray non-numeric
47	// value in the Helm chart cannot crash-loop the controller.
48	func atoiOrDefault(key string, fallback int) int {
49		v := os.Getenv(key)
50		if v == "" {
51			return fallback
52		}
53		n, err := strconv.Atoi(v)
54		if err != nil {
55			return fallback
56		}
57		return n
58	}
59	
60	// resolvePerLevelModels returns the four-key map from level name
61	// ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the model identifier used as the
62	// last-fallback in dispatch_helpers.ResolveProvider (D-C2).
63	//
64	// Defaults match D-C4 — phase brief lock-in:
65	//
66	//	milestone → claude-opus-4-7    (heavy planning, lowest fan-out)
67	//	phase     → claude-sonnet-4-6
68	//	plan      → claude-sonnet-4-6
69	//	task      → claude-haiku-4-5   (highest fan-out, cost-bounded)
70	//
71	// Override per-level via env vars (set by the manager Deployment from Helm
72	// values.yaml subagent.levels.{level}.model):
73	//
74	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE
75	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE
76	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN
77	//	TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK
78	func resolvePerLevelModels() map[string]string {
79		return map[string]string{
80			"milestone": envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_MILESTONE", "claude-opus-4-7"),
81			"phase":     envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PHASE", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
82			"plan":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_PLAN", "claude-sonnet-4-6"),
83			"task":      envOrDefault("TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TASK", "claude-haiku-4-5"),
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// tideHelmProviderDefaults builds the controller.ProviderDefaults struct
88	// (D-C2 last-fallback in ResolveProvider) from the Helm-chart subagent image
89	// + per-level model map. claudeImage is the default subagent image
90	// (CLAUDE_SUBAGENT_IMAGE env var); per-level models come from
91	// resolvePerLevelModels (TIDE_DEFAULT_MODEL_* env vars).
92	//
93	// Called once at Manager startup; the result is stamped onto each up-stack
94	// reconciler's HelmProviderDefaults field (MilestoneReconciler /
95	// PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler).
96	func tideHelmProviderDefaults(claudeImage string) controller.ProviderDefaults {
97		return controller.ProviderDefaults{
98			Image:  claudeImage,
99			Models: resolvePerLevelModels(),
100		}
101	}
102	
103	// resolveLeaderElectionTiming returns the (lease, renew, retry) durations
104	// for controller-runtime's LeaderElection block (D-D1 / Phase 3 chaos-resume).
105	//
106	// Defaults pin to common controller-runtime production values
107	// (lease=15s, renew=10s, retry=2s) which give a single-pod restart ≤25s
108	// failover ceiling, with the invariant lease > renew > retry preserved.
109	// Override via env vars (set by manager Deployment from Helm values.yaml
110	// leaderElection.*):
111	//
112	//	TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS  (default 15)
113	//	TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS  (default 10)
114	//	TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS  (default 2)
115	func resolveLeaderElectionTiming() (lease, renew, retry time.Duration) {
116		lease = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_LEASE_SECONDS", 15)) * time.Second
117		renew = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RENEW_SECONDS", 10)) * time.Second
118		retry = time.Duration(atoiOrDefault("TIDE_LEADER_RETRY_SECONDS", 2)) * time.Second
119		return lease, renew, retry
120	}
121

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive picture of the codebase. Let me now plan the tasks for implementing the Codex subagent.

Based on what I've read, here's what needs to be done:

1. **Task 1: Codex subagent package** - Create `internal/subagent/codex/` with:
   - `doc.go` - package documentation
   - `client.go` - Client struct + constructor
   - `run.go` - Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) implementation
   - `pricing.go` - per-model price table for OpenAI/Codex models
2. **Task 2: Codex Dockerfile** - Create `images/codex-subagent/Dockerfile` mirroring the existing Claude subagent Dockerfile
3. **Task 3: cmd/codex-subagent** - Create the command directory with a main.go that mirrors the Claude subagent's entry point
4. **Task 4: Per-level vendor switch in dispatch_helpers.go** - Fix `ResolveProvider` to infer vendor from the resolved image or add a `Vendor` field to `LevelConfig`

; the prompt confirms this vendor/provider switch is part of the deliverable since currently non-Anthropic images get selected but then rejected at runtime. The cleanest approach is adding a `Vendor` field to both `LevelConfig` and `SubagentConfig` so `ResolveProvider` can read the vendor from the resolved level or infer it from the image reference. Now I'm mapping out the Helm chart updates needed: adding the Codex subagent image configuration, environment variables for the API key reference, and per-level vendor defaults for each milestone type. I also need to update the manager's environment variable handling to support these new configs. Then I'll add unit tests for the Codex subagent with offline guards to ensure reliability.

Looking at the task dependencies, the Codex package functions need to stay together, the vendor switch in dispatch helpers is independent, and everything else builds on top of these foundations. I also need to add the Codex image environment variable and per-level vendor configuration to the Helm chart and manager setup, which depends on the API and dispatch changes. Then I'll add unit tests for the Codex subagent package. I'm also reconsidering whether the auto-generated deepcopy file needs updating—since `Vendor` is just a string field, the controller-gen tool should handle it automatically without manual changes. I need to check the actual API version in the codebase since the prompt mentions v1alpha2 but the project might still be using v1alpha1. Let me verify what's actually in the directory structure.

OK, so it's v1alpha1. For the per-level vendor configuration, adding a `Vendor` field to `LevelConfig` is the straightforward approach. The deepcopy mechanism should handle string fields automatically as part of the struct copy, so I'll add the field to `api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` and let the code generation handle the rest.

Now for the logic: `ResolveProvider` needs to check if `levelCfg.Vendor` is set and use that, otherwise infer from the image name—if it contains "codex" then "openai", otherwise "anthropic". The tricky part is that `ProviderDefaults` currently only carries Image and Models per level, but to support task-level Codex images, I need `ResolveProvider` to return the right vendor when a Codex image is configured at that level. I can either infer the vendor from the image name (if it contains "codex" use OpenAI, otherwise Anthropic) or add an explicit `Vendor` field to `LevelConfig` for operators to control. Since the prompt specifies a per-level vendor switch as a deliverable, I'll go with both approaches—add the field to the schema and use image-name inference as a fallback when it's not explicitly set. The Helm chart defaults would stay as "anthropic" for backward compatibility. Now I'm thinking through the credential handling — the Codex subagent needs `OPENAI_API_KEY` passed in, similar to how the existing credproxy handles `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. The pattern would follow the established Secret reference approach, where the dispatch Job's credproxy sidecar reads the API key from a Kubernetes Secret and makes it available to the subagent. The prompt mentions that chart values for the new Secret name should be part of the deliverable, so I need to add a `codexSubagent.secretRef` key to `values.yaml`. For the credential mechanism, Codex needs the `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var injected directly into the Job pod from a Kubernetes Secret, which is simpler than the credproxy sidecar approach used for Anthropic. I should check how the Job spec is currently built to understand where to inject these environment variables.

content.content:
1	/*
2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3	
4	Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	// Package controller / reporter_jobspec.go — builds the short-lived
18	// tide-reporter reader Job that materializes child CRDs from the PVC-side
19	// out.json into the project namespace (Option C, Phase 09 Plan 06).
20	//
21	// Build pattern mirrors internal/controller/push_helpers.go buildCloneJob:
22	// deterministic-named, project-namespace-scoped, PVC-subPath-mounted,
23	// owner-referenced to the completion-triggering parent. Diverges from
24	// buildCloneJob in three ways:
25	//
26	//  1. SA is "tide-reporter" (NOT "tide-push") — least-privilege create+get
27	//     on the five TIDE CRD Kinds only (reporter-rbac.yaml, plan 09-04).
28	//  2. NO EnvFrom git-creds Secret — reporter needs no PAT.
29	//  3. SecurityContext: RunAsNonRoot + drop ALL capabilities (mirrors
30	//     internal/dispatch/podjob/jobspec.go:368-375 hardened subagent context).
31	//  4. Job + pod template carry tideproject.k8s/role=reporter label so the
32	//     dispatch-completion handler can discriminate reader Jobs from dispatch
33	//     Jobs (T-09-13 re-fire mitigation).
34	package controller
35	
36	import (
37		"fmt"
38	
39		batchv1 "k8s.io/api/batch/v1"
40		corev1 "k8s.io/api/core/v1"
41		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
42		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
43	
44		tideprojectv1alpha1 "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/api/v1alpha1"
45		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/owner"
46	)
47	
48	const (
49		// reporterSAName is the dedicated least-privilege SA for the reader Job pod
50		// (plan 09-04 creates this SA in the project namespace via reporter-rbac.yaml).
51		reporterSAName = "tide-reporter"
52	
53		// reporterContainerName is the single container name in the reader Job spec.
54		reporterContainerName = "reporter"
55	
56		// reporterWorkspaceVolume is the volume name for the per-Project PVC mount.
57		// Mirrors pushWorkspaceVolume (push_helpers.go) for consistency.
58		reporterWorkspaceVolume = "project-workspace"
59	
60		// ReporterRoleLabel is the value of the tideproject.k8s/role label on reader
61		// Jobs. Callers use this constant to discriminate reader Jobs from dispatch Jobs
62		// in the Job-completion handler (T-09-13 mitigation).
63		ReporterRoleLabel = "reporter"
64	)
65	
66	// ReporterOptions carries everything the four planner-completion handlers
67	// supply when constructing the per-parent reader Job (plan 09-06).
68	//
69	// ReporterImage is the image ref for the tide-reporter container
70	// (Helm value images.tideReporter.repository:tag). When empty, the spawn
71	// site should skip creating the Job (mirrors the TidePushImage skip in
72	// boundary_push.go:80-88) — but BuildReporterJob itself always returns a
73	// valid Job so callers control the skip.
74	type ReporterOptions struct {
75		// ReporterImage is the image ref for the tide-reporter container.
76		// PROD_OVERRIDE_REQUIRED: set via TIDE_REPORTER_IMAGE env from Helm value
77		// images.tideReporter.repository:tag. The :v0.1.0-dev tag tracks main
78		// and is NOT a release-stable placeholder.
79		ReporterImage string
80	}
81	
82	// BuildReporterJob constructs the K8s batchv1.Job that runs the in-namespace
83	// reporter process in the project namespace.
84	//
85	// Identity:
86	//
87	//   - Name: "tide-reporter-<parentUID>" — deterministic per parent; AlreadyExists
88	//     on Create is idempotent success (mirrors tide-push-%s / tide-clone-%s naming
89	//     in push_helpers.go:90 / :249). One reader Job per planner-Job completion.
90	//
91	//   - Namespace: project.Namespace — the reporter runs in the TENANT namespace
92	//     so it can read the per-project PVC and call the K8s API to create child CRDs
93	//     under the tide-reporter SA (same-namespace constraint, Pitfall 23).
94	//
95	//   - OwnerReferences: cascade-delete to the parent (Milestone / Phase / Plan /
96	//     Project) via owner.EnsureOwnerRef (Controller=true, BlockOwnerDeletion=true).
97	//
98	// Pod spec:
99	//
100	//   - ServiceAccountName: "tide-reporter" (least-privilege create+get on the
101	//     five TIDE CRD Kinds — plan 09-04 SA+Role+RoleBinding).
102	//
103	//   - PVC subPath: <project.UID>/workspace → /workspace (same layout the dispatch
104	//     Job wrote out.json into — the load-bearing same-namespace fix).
105	//
106	//   - Args: --workspace, --project-uid, --task-uid (envelope key), --parent-name,
107	//     --parent-namespace, --parent-kind. Mirrors plan 09-05 reporter flag set.
108	//
109	//   - SecurityContext: RunAsNonRoot + drop ALL capabilities (mirrors jobspec.go
110	//     hardened subagent context — reporter is trusted write; least-privilege).
111	//     NO AllowPrivilegeEscalation, NO readOnlyRootFilesystem=true (reporter writes
112	//     nothing to its own fs, but container image may need tmpfs — keep false to
113	//     match subagent precedent).
114	//
115	//   - NO EnvFrom git-creds Secret (reporter needs no PAT).
116	//
117	//   - Label tideproject.k8s/role=reporter on both Job and pod template so the
118	//     dispatch-completion watch can discriminate reader Jobs from dispatch Jobs
119	//     (T-09-13 — prevents the handler from re-firing on the reader Job's own
120	//     completion event).
121	func BuildReporterJob(
122		parent metav1.Object,
123		project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project,
124		pvcName string,
125		taskUID string,
126		parentKind string,
127		opts ReporterOptions,
128		scheme *runtime.Scheme,
129	) *batchv1.Job {
130		args := []string{
131			"--workspace=/workspace",
132			"--project-uid=" + string(project.UID),
133			"--task-uid=" + taskUID,
134			"--parent-name=" + parent.GetName(),
135			"--parent-namespace=" + parent.GetNamespace(),
136			"--parent-kind=" + parentKind,
137		}
138	
139		// resolvedPVCName defaults to the project-shared PVC name when the caller
140		// passes an empty string (mirrors defaultSharedPVCName pattern in push_helpers).
141		resolvedPVCName := pvcName
142		if resolvedPVCName == "" {
143			resolvedPVCName = defaultSharedPVCName
144		}
145	
146		// backoffLimitVal + ttlVal: pointers required by the JobSpec API.
147		backoffLimitVal := int32(2)
148		ttlVal := int32(300)
149	
150		// Role label shared by both Job metadata and pod template (T-09-13).
151		roleLabels := map[string]string{
152			"tideproject.k8s/role": ReporterRoleLabel,
153		}
154	
155		// Hardened security context mirroring jobspec.go:368-375.
156		runAsNonRoot := true
157		allowPrivEsc := false
158		sc := &corev1.SecurityContext{
159			RunAsNonRoot:             &runAsNonRoot,
160			AllowPrivilegeEscalation: &allowPrivEsc,
161			Capabilities:             &corev1.Capabilities{Drop: []corev1.Capability{"ALL"}},
162		}
163	
164		job := &batchv1.Job{
165			ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
166				// Deterministic name keyed on the parent UID so that two concurrent
167				// reconcile passes for the same parent both produce the same name and
168				// only one Create succeeds (AlreadyExists = idempotent, D-B5 pattern).
169				Name:      fmt.Sprintf("tide-reporter-%s", parent.GetUID()),
170				Namespace: project.Namespace,
171				Labels:    roleLabels,
172			},
173			Spec: batchv1.JobSpec{
174				BackoffLimit:            &backoffLimitVal,
175				TTLSecondsAfterFinished: &ttlVal,
176				Template: corev1.PodTemplateSpec{
177					ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
178						Labels: roleLabels,
179					},
180					Spec: corev1.PodSpec{
181						RestartPolicy:      corev1.RestartPolicyNever,
182						ServiceAccountName: reporterSAName,
183						Volumes: []corev1.Volume{
184							{
185								Name: reporterWorkspaceVolume,
186								VolumeSource: corev1.VolumeSource{
187									PersistentVolumeClaim: &corev1.PersistentVolumeClaimVolumeSource{
188										ClaimName: resolvedPVCName,
189									},
190								},
191							},
192						},
193						Containers: []corev1.Container{
194							{
195								Name:  reporterContainerName,
196								Image: opts.ReporterImage,
197								Args:  args,
198								VolumeMounts: []corev1.VolumeMount{
199									{
200										Name:      reporterWorkspaceVolume,
201										MountPath: "/workspace",
202										// Same subPath the dispatch Job wrote out.json into —
203										// the load-bearing cross-namespace fix: reader Job is
204										// in the project namespace so it can access the PVC.
205										SubPath: fmt.Sprintf("%s/workspace", project.UID),
206									},
207								},
208								// NO EnvFrom: reporter needs no git credentials PAT.
209								// SecurityContext: hardened, mirrors jobspec.go:368-375.
210								SecurityContext: sc,
211							},
212						},
213					},
214				},
215			},
216		}
217	
218		// Owner ref: parent → Job. EnsureOwnerRef enforces same-namespace (Pitfall 23)
219		// and sets Controller=true + BlockOwnerDeletion=true (CRD-02). Best-effort:
220		// pure builder does not propagate scheme errors (nil scheme in test fixtures).
221		_ = owner.EnsureOwnerRef(job, parent, scheme)
222	
223		return job
224	}
225

For the Codex subagent, I need to handle the planner role by using the `--output-schema` flag to constrain responses to the child-CRD JSON format, similar to how the Anthropic subagent works. The Codex CLI supports `--json` for JSONL streaming, `--output-schema` for schema enforcement, and `--sandbox` for execution context.

The key design question is whether to use the file-handoff pattern (writing JSON files to a `children/` directory like Anthropic does) or leverage Codex's `--output-schema` to emit structured child-CRDs directly to stdout. Since the spec mandates child-CRD format as JSON, I could parse the final stdout directly instead of reading from files, which would be a cleaner approach for Codex's headless execution model.

However, the file-handoff pattern is already well-integrated into the existing architecture—the `readChildCRDs` function handles it seamlessly, and Codex has workspace write capability via `--sandbox workspace-write`, mirroring Claude's edit acceptance model. For consistency and simplicity, I'll stick with the file-handoff approach rather than introducing a different emission mechanism. So for Codex, the cleaner approach is using `--output-schema` to constrain the planner's output to a JSON schema describing the ChildCRDs format, then parsing the structured response directly from stdout instead of scanning files. I'll design run.go to handle both executors with straightforward execution and planners with schema-constrained output parsing. JSON schema approach is getting complicated. I think the simpler path is to stick with the file-handoff method for now — write the child CRDs to disk and read them back, which avoids having to parse complex structured output from the Codex CLI. `readChildCRDs` reads them back, keeping the Codex subagent as a straightforward replacement for the Anthropic one. I should document `--output-schema` as a potential future optimization in the comments. For the JSONL output from `codex exec --json`, I'm looking at how the event stream works—the final result event contains the complete response along with token usage metrics broken down by type.

I'll need to build a stream parser for the Codex JSONL format that extracts the final text and usage information, similar to what I've already done for other formats. When setting up the job spec in `jobspec.go` for OpenAI vendors, I'll skip the credproxy sidecar pattern and inject the API key directly from the secret reference instead.
